sourceMovie.title: Down
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Down (2019) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Daniel Stamm's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Speak No Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Down clicked for you, Speak No Evil (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Down?
faq.answer: Try Speak No Evil, Abigail, P2—each shares DNA with Down while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Down?
faq.answer: Down (2019) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Down worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
